title: tabernaemontana ceratocarpa
intro: Tabernaemontana ceratocarpa, also known as the Ceratocarpa vine or the Yellow Bells, is a plant species native to tropical Africa and parts of Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral compounds. The health benefits of Tabernaemontana ceratocarpa include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain and inflammation, and treat respiratory infections. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which can be used to create teas, tinctures, and other medicinal formulations.
